name: Pappadeaux's Sweet Potato Pecan Pie with Bourbon Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 cup Cooked, mashed sweet 'taters (about 2 medium potatoes)
Unbaked pastry for a single-crust 9"-10 inch pie shell
Pecan filling and Bourbon sauce (ingredients below)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. Combine mashed sweet potatoes, sugars, egg, cream, vanilla, salt, cinnamon, allspice, nutmeg, and butter in an electric mixing bowl. Beat at medium-low speed until smooth; do not overmix.
3. To assemble pie, spoon the sweet potato filling into the pastry-lined pie pan. Fill shell evenly to the top with pecan filling.
4. Bake for 1 1/2 hours or until a knife inserted into the center of the pie comes out clean. Store pie at room temperature for 24 hours.
5. Serve pie slices with Bourbon Sauce on top or to the side.
Pecan Pie Filling:
1. Combine sugar, syrup, eggs, butter, vanilla, salt, and cinnamon in an electric mixing bowl. Beat on low speed until syrup is opaque (about 4-5 minutes). Stir in pecans and mix well.
Bourbon Sauce:
1. Combine cream and milk in a large mixing bowl.
2. Slowly whip in pudding mix. Add bourbon and continue whipping. Add vanilla and whip until mixture is well blended to sauce consistency (should not be as firm as pudding, but should not be runny). Sauce should be made about one hour before use; it will thicken as it sits.
